shell.freePlanBanner: Free plan: explore setup with one small workspace. Upgrade only when you need higher limits for frameworks, integrations, users, or customer-facing proof.

authFallback.signUpBody: Add the Clerk keys to the environment to enable registration and organisations.

appDataNotice.unavailableBody: Live organisation data could not be loaded. Demo data is not shown in production.

billingSettings.stripeMissing: Stripe keys are not configured in this environment yet.

starter.name: Starter

business.name: Business

consultant.name: Consultant
consultant.description: Unlimited client workspaces and white-label reporting.

billingSettings.billingUnavailable: Checkout and portal actions are disabled in this environment until Stripe billing keys are configured. Your local plan limits are still shown for planning.

frameworkWizard.error: The assessment could not be saved.
frameworkWizard.resultBody: The result was saved to control statuses and the dashboard was recalculated.
frameworkWizard.intro: Answers set the initial control statuses and prepare a gap report.

mfa.text: Is MFA required for all user accounts?
mfa.help: Include primary identity, administrators, and remote access.

privileged_access.help: At least quarterly or when roles change.

guest_access.text: Are external and guest accounts controlled and removed?
guest_access.help: Inactive guests should be detected and revoked.

training.text: Do employees complete regular security training?
training.help: Onboarding training and annual refreshers with records.

incident_plan.text: Do you have an approved incident response plan?
incident_plan.help: Include roles, escalation, communications, and review.

incident_reporting.text: Can you track and meet 72-hour notification deadlines?
incident_reporting.help: GDPR and NIS2 have strict incident reporting deadlines.

encryption.text: Is sensitive data encrypted at rest?

patching.text: Do you run a managed patch management process?
patching.help: Track critical vulnerabilities, deadlines, and exceptions.

backups.text: Do you test restore from backups?
backups.help: Creating backups is not enough; restore must be verified regularly.

logging.text: Do you collect security logs and alerts?
logging.help: Cover cloud, identity, endpoints, and critical applications.

suppliers.text: Do you assess supplier security and contractual requirements?
suppliers.help: NIS2 and ISO 27001 both require supply-chain controls.

asset_inventory.text: Do you maintain a current inventory of assets and systems?
asset_inventory.help: Include SaaS, cloud, endpoints, applications, and responsible people.

business_continuity.text: Do you have a documented business continuity plan?
business_continuity.help: Include responsible people, recovery objectives, and dependencies.

secure_baseline.text: Do you use secure configuration baselines for systems?
secure_baseline.help: For example hardened settings for M365, AWS, or endpoints.

network_segmentation.text: Are critical systems segmented from the network?
network_segmentation.help: Segmentation limits the impact of compromise.

change_management.text: Are changes to critical systems approved and traceable?
change_management.help: Include configuration, infrastructure, and application changes.

penetration_test.text: Do you run at least annual penetration testing or security audit?
penetration_test.help: Focus on internet-facing systems and critical processes.

endpoint_protection.text: Are endpoints protected with EDR/AV and centrally managed?
endpoint_protection.help: Cover company laptops, servers, and mobile devices.

ai_inventory.text: Do you maintain an inventory of AI systems in use?
ai_inventory.help: Record purpose, supplier, owner, data, and risk category.

ai_literacy.text: Do employees working with AI complete AI literacy training?
ai_literacy.help: Article 4 applies broadly to deployers and providers.

prohibited_practices.text: Have you checked that AI is not used for prohibited practices?
prohibited_practices.help: For example social scoring or workplace emotion recognition.

high_risk_classification.text: Do you classify whether AI falls into high-risk categories?
high_risk_classification.help: HR, education, credit, biometrics, and critical infrastructure.

provider_verification.text: Do you verify compliance of high-risk AI providers?
provider_verification.help: CE marking, EU declaration of conformity, and instructions for use.

human_oversight.text: Is human oversight defined for AI decisions?
human_oversight.help: A responsible person must be able to assess, interrupt, or reverse decisions.

ai_logs.text: Do you retain high-risk AI usage logs for at least 6 months?
ai_logs.help: Include automatic logs and manual records of use.

individual_notice.text: Do you inform affected people about AI-assisted decision-making?
individual_notice.help: Especially for HR, customer decisions, and public services.

content_labeling.text: Do you label AI-generated content where required?
content_labeling.help: Chatbots, synthetic content, and deepfake outputs.

data_governance.text: Do you have rules for input data, personal data, and sensitive information in AI?
data_governance.help: Connect AI governance with GDPR and data classification.

incident_monitoring.text: Can you detect and handle incidents or harmful AI outputs?
incident_monitoring.help: Include escalation, corrective actions, and audit trail.

supplier_contracts.text: Do AI suppliers have contractual security and compliance requirements?
supplier_contracts.help: Document roles, data, responsibilities, and termination terms.

ropa.text: Do you maintain records of personal data processing activities?

privacy_notice.text: Is your privacy notice current and understandable?
privacy_notice.help: Describe purposes, legal bases, recipients, and data subject rights.

dsr.text: Do you have a process for data subject requests?
dsr.help: Access, erasure, rectification, portability, and objection.

retention.text: Do you have a retention schedule and data deletion rules?
retention.help: Personal data should not be kept longer than necessary.

dpia.text: Do you perform DPIAs for risky processing?
dpia.help: For example monitoring, profiling, or large-scale sensitive data.

processors.text: Do processors have DPAs and security requirements in place?
processors.help: Suppliers processing personal data need contractual controls.

classification.text: Do you classify personal and sensitive data in systems?
classification.help: This helps manage access, encryption, and retention rules.

physical_access.text: Are offices and sensitive areas physically protected?
physical_access.help: Access, visitors, and physical media records.

media_disposal.text: Do you dispose of media and devices securely?
media_disposal.help: Disk wiping, shredding, and disposal records.

crypto_policy.text: Do you have a cryptography and key management policy?

code_review.text: Do you require code review and main branch protection?
code_review.help: Applies to development teams and critical repositories.

secrets.text: Do you manage secrets and detect leaks in code?
secrets.help: Secret scanning, token rotation, and least privilege.

dependency_alerts.help: Dependency alerts, SBOM, or regular vulnerability scans.

cloud_audit.text: Do you have cloud audit logs and baseline security controls?
cloud_audit.help: CloudTrail, root MFA, S3 encryption, or equivalents.

change_management_iso.help: Approval, testing, rollback, and change owner.

isms_governance.text: Have you defined ISMS scope, SoA, and a risk treatment plan?
isms_governance.help: A certification audit will expect scope, Statement of Applicability, and linkage between risks and controls.

isms_review.text: Do internal audits and management reviews take place?
isms_review.help: ISO 27001 requires a demonstrable cycle of internal audits, corrections, and management review.

document_control.text: Do you control documentation and approve exceptions?
document_control.help: Policies, procedures, exceptions, and records need an owner, version, and review.

remote_and_mobile.text: Are mobile devices and remote work security-managed?
remote_and_mobile.help: Include MDM, encryption, remote wipe, VPN, and home-working rules.

materiality.text: Have you completed double materiality and approved ESG priorities?
materiality.help: CSRD requires assessing company impacts and the financial effect of ESG topics on the company.

emissions.text: Do you measure Scope 1, Scope 2, and relevant Scope 3 emissions?
emissions.help: Each number needs a clear data source, method, and owner.

resource_use.text: Do you track energy, water, waste, and environmental incidents?
resource_use.help: Core environmental data must be auditable and regularly updated.

environmental_risk.text: Do you assess climate risks and biodiversity impacts?
environmental_risk.help: If a topic is not relevant, keep the rationale in the materiality assessment.

workforce.text: Do you have workforce, health and safety, training, and diversity data?
workforce.help: Social metrics must respect local employment and privacy limits.

supply_chain.text: Do you assess ESG risk in the supply chain?
supply_chain.help: Supplier questionnaires and due diligence help answer enterprise customers.

governance.text: Do you have business conduct policy and anti-corruption training?
governance.help: Governance covers ethics, conflicts of interest, corruption, and approvals.

report_approval.text: Is the ESG report approved with an auditable data trail?
report_approval.help: Before export, it must be clear who supplied, verified, and approved the data.

metrics.scoreBody: Average of framework scores and control statuses.

frameworkDescriptions.iso27001: Information security management system and security control catalogue.
frameworkDescriptions.csrd: ESG reporting, double materiality, data evidence, and supplier questionnaires.

demoRisks.title: MFA is not enforced on key accounts
demoRisks.description: MFA is not enforced for every cloud and administrator account.

demoRisks.title: Backup recovery is not verified
demoRisks.description: Backups are not tested regularly and recovery may not meet RTO.

demoRisks.title: Supplier security is not managed
demoRisks.description: A critical SaaS supplier has no security assessment or DPA on file.

demoRisks.title: Incident response is not ready
demoRisks.description: Incident response roles are not rehearsed and reporting could exceed 72h.

demoRisks.title: Personal data retention is unmanaged
demoRisks.description: Personal data is stored in tools without clear retention and access rules.

demoRisks.title: Endpoint management is weak
demoRisks.description: Laptops do not have consistent encryption, EDR, and patch management.

demoRisks.title: Access reviews are irregular
demoRisks.description: Access rights for former employees and suppliers are not reviewed consistently.

demoRisks.title: AI tool usage is uncontrolled
demoRisks.description: Employees use AI tools without rules for sensitive data.

demoRisks.title: Audit trail coverage is insufficient
demoRisks.description: Audit logs are not centralised or do not have enough retention.

demoRisks.title: Security training evidence is missing
demoRisks.description: Security training cannot be evidenced for every employee.

questions.access_control: Does the vendor use MFA and regular access reviews?

questions.backup_recovery: Does the vendor test backups and disaster recovery?

questions.business_continuity: Does the vendor have a business continuity plan?
questions.data_location: Does the vendor keep data in the EU/EEA or under a valid transfer mechanism?
questions.termination: Can the vendor securely return or delete data after contract termination?

footer.tagline: Early access platform for EU compliance automation.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.body: We use required cookies and optional traffic measurement to improve Splnit.eu.

earlyAccess.title: Looking for our first 10 design partners.
earlyAccess.body: Splnit.eu is in early access. It is a founder-led platform built in Olomouc for companies that want to shape the product before public launch.
earlyAccess.emptyProof: No public references are listed yet. When they exist, they will go on the site only with the customer's permission.
earlyAccess.onboardingTitle: Three weeks on a real project.
earlyAccess.onboardingBody: The goal is not a demo account. The goal is to use Splnit.eu on a real NIS2 or GDPR problem and find what the product still misses.

benefits.businessFree: 12 months free on the Business plan
benefits.guidedOnboarding: 3 weeks of guided onboarding directly with the founder
benefits.directContact: Direct contact without a ticket queue

asks.realUse: Real use on your NIS2 or GDPR project
asks.weeklyFeedback: 30 minutes of weekly feedback during onboarding
asks.referencePermission: Permission to use your name as a reference only when you are happy

weeks.week1Body: We confirm jurisdiction, walk through NIS2 applicability, and prepare the first priority list.
weeks.week2Body: We connect Microsoft 365, AWS, or GitHub and configure the first automated checks.
weeks.week3Body: We generate the first document set and prepare an evidence format for your auditor.

about.title: Who's behind Splnit.eu.
about.body: Splnit.eu is a focused, founder-led platform built by a senior IT engineer with deep experience in European compliance frameworks.
about.notYetTitle: What we do not claim yet
about.whyP1: Large global compliance platforms often cover generic frameworks well, but EU SMB teams also need local transpositions, clear citations, and practical onboarding.
about.whyP2: Splnit.eu starts smaller: automated checks for systems companies already use, documents ready for legal review, and direct founder work with the first design partners.

realToday.solo: Splnit.eu is a founder-led early access product built in Olomouc.
realToday.focus: The product focuses on EU compliance automation for NIS2, GDPR, and ISO 27001.
realToday.legalReview: The DPA and final legal documents are ready for legal review before production launch.
realToday.noFakeProof: Public references and metrics are not shown until they are verified.

notYet.legalAdvice: This is not legal advice, an audit opinion, or certification.
notYet.enterprise: Splnit.eu is not yet an enterprise vendor with a 24/7 SLA and penalties.
notYet.certification: ISO 27001 or SOC 2 certification for Splnit.eu itself is not publicly claimed.
notYet.entity: Operator identification details are published on the legal pages before any contract is signed.

home.lead: NIS2, EU AI Act, GDPR, and ISO 27001 with the regulatory citations your local auditor expects. Built for European SMBs in Italian, Czech, and English.
home.trustLine: Early access · Direct founder contact · Vercel + Neon infrastructure
home.stepsTitle: Set it once. Keep it running.
home.stepsBody: Three steps from first system connection to a usable evidence pack.
home.featuresTitle: Compliance built for IT and compliance teams.
home.featuresBody: Connect local and global infrastructure. Controls are mapped to local and international standards.
home.offerBody: Splnit.eu is in early access. The first design partners get the Business plan free for 12 months and three weeks of guided onboarding directly with the founder.
home.referencePlaceholder: Customer references will be published with explicit consent as design partners complete onboarding.
home.finalBody: Become a design partner and help build the product around a real NIS2 or GDPR project.

steps.connectBody: Connect Microsoft 365, GitHub, or AWS. Setup takes 5 minutes.
steps.testsBody: Automated checks monitor connected systems and save results as audit evidence.
steps.resultsBody: Dashboard, failure alerts, and audit documentation prepared for your auditor.

features.residencyBody: Production uses Vercel for hosting and Neon Postgres for the database. Sub-processors and processing context are listed in the DPA overview.
features.monitoringBody: Catch configuration drift in Microsoft 365 or AWS immediately.
features.documentsBody: Generate working documents and evidence packs that can go to a lawyer, auditor, or consultant for final review.

leadCapture.title: Which EU regulations apply to you?
leadCapture.subtitle: Get a personalised obligations overview in 2 minutes.
leadCapture.success: Sent. Your overview will arrive within 2 minutes.
leadCapture.footnote: No credit card · Immediate results · Unsubscribe anytime

pricing.title: Transparent pricing. No surprises.
pricing.subtitle: Less than one consultant hour per month.
pricing.footnote: All plans include EU data storage · No hidden fees · Cancel anytime
pricing.partnerTitle: Are you a consultant or MSP?
pricing.partnerBody: Unlimited clients, white-label option, partner badge.
pricing.finalBody: Early access is open for the first design partners who want to use Splnit.eu on a real NIS2 or GDPR project.

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: For companies starting structured compliance work.
features: Automated checks for Microsoft 365, AWS, and GitHub

business.name: Business
business.description: For teams managing compliance across frameworks.

faqs.cancelAnswer: Yes. There are no annual commitments on the monthly plan. For annual plans, we refund the proportional part.
faqs.dataQuestion: Is my data stored in the EU?
faqs.dataAnswer: The production setup is designed for EU regions. Exact processing locations and any transfer mechanisms outside the EU/EEA are confirmed in the DPA and sub-processor list.
faqs.setupQuestion: Do I need technical knowledge to set it up?
faqs.setupAnswer: No. The setup wizard takes 15 minutes without an IT department.
faqs.legalAnswer: No. The platform helps you prepare for legal review; it does not replace a qualified lawyer.
faqs.discountQuestion: Do you offer discounts for non-profits or startups?
faqs.discountAnswer: Yes. Contact hello@splnit.eu for special terms.
faqs.cancelDataQuestion: What happens to my data after cancellation?
faqs.cancelDataAnswer: You have 30 days to export all data. After that, it is permanently deleted.

regulations.title: Which EU regulations apply to you?
regulations.subtitle: A plain-language guide to EU obligations for SMBs, with deadlines, enforcement risk, and practical next steps.
regulations.downloadSubtitle: Obligations overview, document templates, and checklists for each regulation.

frameworks.title: Choose frameworks

descriptions.nis2: Cybersecurity, risk management, incident reporting, and management accountability.
descriptions.ai-act: AI deployer obligations, AI literacy, high-risk AI, and transparency requirements.
descriptions.gdpr: Personal data protection, records of processing activities, security, and breach notification.
descriptions.iso27001: Information security management system and security control catalogue.
descriptions.csrd: ESG reporting, double materiality, data evidence, and supplier questionnaires.

frameworks.body: Pick the obligations you want Splnit.eu to turn into controls. You can add more later; start with the framework you would discuss first with a prospect or auditor.

tools.title: AI tools and SaaS inventory
tools.body: Select the systems you actually use. This shapes the first integration recommendation and keeps the setup grounded in your environment.

integration.title: Recommended integration
integration.body: The first automated checks cover MFA, Conditional Access, guests, and privileged roles.
integration.optional: This is a recommended next action, not a blocker. You can review the integration scope now and connect it after the workspace is ready.

score.label: Initial score
score.title: The first baseline is ready
score.body: The score is based on selected frameworks and tool inventory. Automated tests will refine it after integrations are connected.
score.nextStep: After finishing, start on the dashboard: review active frameworks, open controls, and the first integration step.

organisationSettings.title: Organisation
organisationSettings.description: Basic organisation identity, classification, and Clerk organisation link.

nis2.title: NIS2 classification
nis2.description: Indicative internal label based on sector and size. Final legal assessment stays outside automated classification.

categories.essential_or_important_entity_review: Essential or important entity review needed

questionnairePage.title: Security questionnaires
questionnairePage.subtitle: Draft answers to customer questionnaires using passing controls, evidence, and policies already stored in Splnit.eu.
questionnairePage.disclaimer: Generated answers are drafts for legal and compliance review. They are not legal advice, certification, or a guarantee of compliance.
questionnairePage.disabledNotice: AI questionnaires require Clerk, DATABASE_URL, QUESTIONNAIRE_AI_ENABLED=true, and a configured AI provider.

metrics.controlsBody: Used as the primary answer context.
metrics.evidenceBody: Automated evidence can support high confidence.

history.title: Saved outputs
history.subtitle: Recent questionnaire answers and gap reports stored for this organisation.
history.empty: No generated outputs have been saved yet.

workbench.emptyBody: Generated answers will be grounded in passing controls, evidence, and active policies from {organisation}.
sampleQuestions: Do you enforce MFA for all users?
Do you have an incident response policy and breach notification process?
How do you manage vendor security risk?
Are access reviews performed at least quarterly?
Do you retain audit logs for security events?

actionErrors.authRequired: Sign-in and an active organisation are required.
actionErrors.invalidInput: Paste questionnaire text or upload a text file.
actionErrors.missingConfig: AI questionnaires are not enabled or the provider is not configured.
actionErrors.noQuestions: No questions were found in the questionnaire.
actionErrors.rateLimit: The monthly limit of {limit} questionnaires has been reached.

unsupported.summary: No supported compliance context is available for this organisation yet.
unsupported.answer: No answer can be generated from the current Splnit.eu organisation data.
unsupported.notes: Add passing controls, evidence, active policies, or reviewed legal citations before using this answer externally.
